channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: Community fights back against caravan park development proposal
item.description: A community group in Victoria's south-east will appeal a council planning decision to approve the development of a regional caravan park, which it says would be an "absolute disaster" for the region.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Pope, Lily Kristanto, and Rachael Lucas

media:group.media:description: Daniel Gunton says it is disappointing caravanners and campers will not be able to stay in the town.

item.title: Live: ASX set to jump on Iran peace hopes
item.description: The futures market tips the ASX 200 to jump more than 1 per cent as a shaky peace holds in the Middle East. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Ziffer

media:group.media:description: ASX Board. Bridge st Sydney

item.title: Shops gutted after suspicious fire at Melbourne butcher and grocery
item.description: Fire crews were called to a blaze in Glenroy that gutted several shops. Locals say there have been multiple suspicious fires in the area.

media:group.media:description: Firefighters were called to the scene about 2.40am and had to force their way into the shop to extinguish the blaze.

item.title: Live: Concerns raised tobacco giant allowed to push for excise cut in private
item.description: A public health academic has raised concerns a tobacco giant was allowed to lobby for a cut to the tobacco excise in private. Follow live.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Boscaini

media:group.media:description: A man smokes a cigarette, May 2024.

item.title: PM knocks back $94 million proposal to help flooded farmers restock
item.description: Widespread flooding in north-west Queensland killed more than 90,000 head of livestock this year, but farmers will have to go it alone if they want to rebuild.
item.dc:creator: Abbey Halter, Tim Little, and Zara Margolis

media:group.media:description: It's estimated up to 100,000 animals perished in months of relentless flooding, though the true figure won't be known until mustering is complete.

item.title: Breaking: Stranded students rescued from flooding in national park
item.description: Almost 50 high school students and teachers have been rescued after becoming stranded by floodwater at a national park in the Scenic Rim.

media:group.media:description: Almost 50 high school teachers and students were safely rescued from Mount Barney National Park overnight.

item.title: Calls for hastily constructed NT evacuation site to be relocated
item.description: Residents in the regional NT town of Batchelor say they have been left in the dark about the future of a pop-up evacuation centre that was built earlier this year without their consultation for flood evacuees.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Hathaway-Wilson

media:group.media:description: About 30 portable buildings and accompanying power generators have been erected in Batchelor to house flood evacuees from the remote community of Palumpa.

item.title: Cummins stars as Sunrisers Hyderabad book spot in IPL playoffs
item.description: Pat Cummins delivers when his team needs it most with crucial wickets as Sunrisers Hyderabad beat Chennai Super Kings.

media:group.media:description: Pat Cummins came up big when it mattered most as Sunrisers Hyderabad secured a big win against Chennai Super Kings.

item.title: Demand for financial help rising among cancer patients, charity says
item.description: Research from the Cancer Council shows more than 38 per cent of cancer patients lose their income during treatment. For Catalina Alcazar, it was an unexpected extra burden as she faced treatment for a tumour in her oesophagus.
item.dc:creator: Lily Nothling

media:group.media:description: Catalina Alcazar's cancer diagnosis at 39 upended her life.

item.title: There's no cure and no treatment for MND, but finally there is hope
item.description: Natalie has seen her body and speech deteriorate; she has lost her independence and dignity — but she says new research into motor neurone disease has given her hope for those diagnosed in the future.
item.dc:creator: Elly Bradfield

media:group.media:description: Natalie Verdouw says her life has dramatically changed since her MND diagnosis in January 2025.

item.title: Breaking: Five dead in shooting at San Diego mosque, including two suspects
item.description: A shooting at a San Diego mosque has left three men dead as well as two teenage suspects, police say.

media:group.media:description: San Diego police have "neutralised" a shooter at the county's largest mosque.

item.title: What Kylie Minogue learned from Michael Hutchence and Nick Cave
item.description: Australia's reigning Queen of Pop discusses her relationships with Nick Cave and the late Michael Hutchence, and being tapped to headline the AFL grand final's pre-game entertainment.
item.dc:creator: Al Newstead

media:group.media:description: Kylie Minogue opens up about Nick Cave, Michael Hutchence and much more in a new three-part documentary series.

item.title: National Trust SA faces inquiry motion amid $230,000 property dispute
item.description: Another National of Trust South Australia branch goes public about its financial dispute with the high-profile heritage group, as the state opposition announces it will refer members' concerns to a parliamentary committee.
item.dc:creator: George Yankovich and Samuel O'Brien

media:group.media:description: Barry Long at the Millicent Museum.

item.title: Woman dies after being hit by ute on Pacific Motorway
item.description: Police say initial enquiries indicate a female was out of her vehicle when she was struck.

media:group.media:description: Any witnesses to a multi-car crash on the Pacific Motorway are urged to contact police.

item.title: Granny flat ban lifted for some residents under airport flight path
item.description: The NSW government will permit secondary dwellings such as granny flats in some areas affected by noise from Western Sydney International Airport.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Chandler

media:group.media:description: Secondary dwellings were banned in LGAs such as Wollondilly and Penrith to mitigate noise impacts and population growth.

item.title: Behind the front lines of the war against illegal waste
item.description: While Queensland councils cleaned up enough illegally dumped waste to fill more than 1,200 garbage trucks, investigators say they are getting better at catching those doing the wrong thing.
item.dc:creator: Charlie McLean

media:group.media:description: Mark Bolton investigates reports of illegal waste dumping in Ipswich.

item.title: The dead fish waste disaster that Tasmania failed to plan for
item.description: High-volume salmon mortalities last year swamped Tasmania's waste streams as companies scrambled to find somewhere to accept them, documents show. There are now concerns the state remains unprepared for future mass deaths.
item.dc:creator: Adam Holmes

media:group.media:description: Salmon in bins, prior to being processed or disposed of during the mortality event.

item.title: A secret mouse experiment in the 1990s threatened Brisbane's water supply
item.description: Brisbane was days away from running out of drinkable water in the 1990s due to a secret mouse experiment that was kept hidden from the public … until now.
item.dc:creator: Craig Zonca and Loretta Ryan and Kenji Sato

media:group.media:description: Jim Soorley says the secret was kept strictly under wraps.

item.title: Trump says he has postponed a planned new strike on Iran
item.description: US President Donald Trump says an attack on Iran that was "scheduled for tomorrow" has been postponed at the request of Gulf state leaders.
item.dc:creator: Brad Ryan

media:group.media:description: Donald Trump has been trying to extract an acceptable "deal" from Iran for weeks.

item.title: It's time for Eric Abetz to tackle Tasmania's debt. How far will he go?
item.description: The lead-up to state budget day is normally a time for flashy funding announcements. But as Tasmania stares down the barrel of $7 billion of debt, things are looking a bit bleaker.
item.dc:creator: Josh Duggan

media:group.media:description: This year will be Eric Abetz's first major budget after only small tweaks post-election last year.

item.title: The parts of Victoria adding the biggest share to disaster damage bills
item.description: At least $57 billion worth of infrastructure in Victoria is vulnerable to more frequent and severe bushfires, floods and extreme heat. This figure is expected to grow.
item.dc:creator: Alex Lim and Sacha Payne

item.title: 'Two years to live': Why we used our super for weight loss surgery
item.description: Weight loss surgery has become an increasingly common option for people grappling with obesity — and many are funding it using retirement savings.
item.dc:creator: Johnson Raela

media:group.media:description: Fili Fili and Natasha Narayan-Fili both had weight loss surgery in 2025.

item.title: 'The party had to end': Young investors torn on budget tax reform
item.description: Some young Australians locked out of the property market due to unaffordable housing have pursued ETFs and "rentvesting" as a way to build financial security. Here's what they make of the budget's CGT reforms.
item.dc:creator: Lin Lin

media:group.media:description: Young ETF investor Vanessa says the budget is a "step in the right direction" but expects to take a financial hit.

item.title: Should a nurse pay the same tax rate as their employer? The budget says no
item.description: As the federal budget prompts howls of protest from those who earn the bulk of their income from owning assets rather than working, we shouldn't forget the relative privilege that has allowed many of those people to take those risks.
item.dc:creator: Michael Janda

media:group.media:description: It's easier to take a gamble on an investment if you are already privileged.

item.title: 'I wish I were dead': The heartbreaking toll of Australia's favourite pastime
item.description: Australians are the world's biggest gamblers, losing billions every year. But the true cost goes far beyond the financial.
item.dc:creator: Anna Levy

media:group.media:description: Gambling is often considered a national pastime in Australia.
